briefdescription.para: Doubly-linked list structure.
Doubly-linked list node structure.
initialize list to its empty state

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list

briefdescription.para: initialize node to its state when not in a list

parameterdescription.para: the node

briefdescription.para: check if a node is a member of any list

parameterdescription.para: the node

simplesect.para: true if node is linked into a list, false if it is not

briefdescription.para: check if a node is the list's head

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the node to check

simplesect.para: true if node is the head, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: check if a node is the list's tail

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the node to check

simplesect.para: true if node is the tail, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: check if the list is empty

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on

briefdescription.para: check if more than one node present

detaileddescription.para: This and other sys_dlist_*() functions are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on

simplesect.para: true if multiple nodes, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: get a reference to the head item in the list

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on

simplesect.para: a pointer to the head element, NULL if list is empty

briefdescription.para: get a reference to the head item in the list

detaileddescription.para: The list must be known to be non-empty.

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on

simplesect.para: a pointer to the head element

briefdescription.para: get a reference to the next item in the list, node is not NULL

detaileddescription.para: Faster than sys_dlist_peek_next() if node is known not to be NULL.

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the node from which to get the next element in the list

simplesect.para: a pointer to the next element from a node, NULL if node is the tail

briefdescription.para: get a reference to the next item in the list

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the node from which to get the next element in the list

simplesect.para: a pointer to the next element from a node, NULL if node is the tail or NULL (when node comes from reading the head of an empty list).

briefdescription.para: get a reference to the previous item in the list, node is not NULL

detaileddescription.para: Faster than sys_dlist_peek_prev() if node is known not to be NULL.

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the node from which to get the previous element in the list

simplesect.para: a pointer to the previous element from a node, NULL if node is the tail

briefdescription.para: get a reference to the previous item in the list

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the node from which to get the previous element in the list

simplesect.para: a pointer to the previous element from a node, NULL if node is the tail or NULL (when node comes from reading the head of an empty list).

briefdescription.para: get a reference to the tail item in the list

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on

simplesect.para: a pointer to the tail element, NULL if list is empty

briefdescription.para: add node to tail of list

detaileddescription.para: This and other sys_dlist_*() functions are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the element to append

briefdescription.para: add node to head of list

detaileddescription.para: This and other sys_dlist_*() functions are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the element to append

briefdescription.para: Insert a node into a list.

detaileddescription.para: Insert a node before a specified node in a dlist.

parameterdescription.para: the position before which "node" will be inserted
the element to insert

briefdescription.para: insert node at position

detaileddescription.para: Insert a node in a location depending on a external condition. The cond() function checks if the node is to be inserted before the current node against which it is checked. This and other sys_dlist_*() functions are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on
the element to insert
a function that determines if the current node is the correct insert point
parameter to cond()

briefdescription.para: remove a specific node from a list

detaileddescription.para: Like :c:func:sys_dlist_remove(), this routine removes a specific node from a list. However, unlike :c:func:sys_dlist_remove(), this routine does not re-initialize the removed node. One significant implication of this difference is that the function :c:funcsys_dnode_is_linked() will not work on a dequeued node.
The list is implicit from the node. The node must be part of a list. This and other sys_dlist_*() functions are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: the node to dequeue

briefdescription.para: remove a specific node from a list

detaileddescription.para: The list is implicit from the node. The node must be part of a list. This and other sys_dlist_*() functions are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: the node to remove

briefdescription.para: Move a range of nodes to the head of the specified list.

detaileddescription.para: This routine moves a range of nodes from their current list to the head of the list dest. The range of nodes is defined by the pointers start and last, which point to the first and last nodes in the range, respectively.
This and other sys_dlist_*() functions are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: the list into which the range of nodes will be moved
the first node in the range to be moved
the last node in the range to be moved

briefdescription.para: Move a range of nodes to the end of the specified list.

detaileddescription.para: This routine moves a range of nodes from their current list to the end of the list dest. The range of nodes is defined by the pointers start and last, which point to the first and last nodes in the range, respectively.

parameterdescription.para: the list into which the range of nodes will be moved
the first node in the range to be moved
the last node in the range to be moved

briefdescription.para: get the first node in a list

detaileddescription.para: This and other sys_dlist_*() functions are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: the doubly-linked list to operate on

simplesect.para: the first node in the list, NULL if list is empty

briefdescription.para: Compute the size of the given list in O(n) time.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer on the list

simplesect.para: an integer equal to the size of the list, or 0 if empty

briefdescription.para: Provide the primitive to iterate on a list Note: the loop is unsafe and thus __dn should not be removed.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer on a sys_dlist_t to iterate on
A sys_dnode_t pointer to peek each node of the list

briefdescription.para: Provide the primitive to iterate on a list, from a node in the list Note: the loop is unsafe and thus __dn should not be removed.

detaileddescription.para: This and other SYS_DLIST_*() macros are not thread safe.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer on a sys_dlist_t to iterate on
A sys_dnode_t pointer to peek each node of the list; it contains the starting node, or NULL to start from the head

briefdescription.para: Provide the primitive to safely iterate on a list Note: __dn can be removed, it will not break the loop.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer on a sys_dlist_t to iterate on
A sys_dnode_t pointer to peek each node of the list
A sys_dnode_t pointer for the loop to run safely

briefdescription.para: Provide the primitive to resolve the container of a list node Note: it is safe to use with NULL pointer nodes.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer on a sys_dnode_t to get its container
Container struct type pointer
The field name of sys_dnode_t within the container struct

briefdescription.para: Provide the primitive to peek container of the list head.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer on a sys_dlist_t to peek
Container struct type pointer
The field name of sys_dnode_t within the container struct

briefdescription.para: Provide the primitive to peek the next container.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer on a sys_dlist_t to peek
Container struct type pointer
The field name of sys_dnode_t within the container struct

briefdescription.para: Provide the primitive to iterate on a list under a container Note: the loop is unsafe and thus __cn should not be detached.

detaileddescription.para:
User MUST add the loop statement curly braces enclosing its own code: SYS_DLIST_FOR_EACH_CONTAINER(l, c, n) { <user code> }
__dl
A pointer on a sys_dlist_t to iterate on
__cn
A container struct type pointer to peek each entry of the list
__n
The field name of sys_dnode_t within the container struct

briefdescription.para: Provide the primitive to safely iterate on a list under a container Note: __cn can be detached, it will not break the loop.

detaileddescription.para:
User MUST add the loop statement curly braces enclosing its own code: SYS_DLIST_FOR_EACH_CONTAINER_SAFE(l, c, cn, n) { <user code> }
__dl
A pointer on a sys_dlist_t to iterate on
__cn
A container struct type pointer to peek each entry of the list
__cns
A container struct type pointer for the loop to run safely
__n
The field name of sys_dnode_t within the container struct

briefdescription.para: Static initializer for a doubly-linked list.
Doubly-linked list implementation.

detaileddescription.para: Doubly-linked list implementation using inline macros/functions. This API is not thread safe, and thus if a list is used across threads, calls to functions must be protected with synchronization primitives.

The lists are expected to be initialized such that both the head and tail pointers point to the list itself. Initializing the lists in such a fashion simplifies the adding and removing of nodes to/from the list.
